game.short_text: Make sure nothing goes wrong as a new player stumbles through a platformer...
game.title: Blissfully Unaware

details.title: Blissfully Unaware
details.description:
Heads up - This has been updated since it was rated at the end of the Game Jam. You can find the original as a windows download at the bottom of the page.

Play with your mouse by moving objects in the levels! 
[Left Mouse Button] = Grab object
[R] = Reset level
[S] = Skip level
[ESC] = Pause Menu


You take charge in making sure nothing goes wrong for a new player as they stumble their way through the levels of a platformer. Frantically modify the environment around them to ensure their first playing experience doesn't leave bad memories!


Rotating platform - The platform doesn't rotate by itself, you need to find another way...Boss fight Stage 1 - Move the lasers out of the way since they fire quickly. Then, you need to hold the bombs close to the core to deal damage. 



THANK YOU SO MUCH TO EVERYONE WHO PLAYED MY GAME! I could never have imagined the turnout, and I am eternally grateful to all of you! I make games because I love seeing how people can take an environment and produce so many unique and awesome ways to explore it. I am so happy that I am able to make something that other people can enjoy! Thank you again to everyone who played my game in the jam or is playing it now. You are what motivate me to keep following this passion. - 0FreshBean
This game was made in a week for the 2025.1 Brackeys Game Jam.
Within 48 hours after the submission time ended, I fixed general bugs as well as issues from porting to Web. Version 1.1.11 is the latest version before submission closed.
Bugs fixed:
UI completely disappearing when pressing fullscreen before the game startsVoice lines playing immediately when entering levelsUI overlapping and going off the screen on some resolutionsBouncy platform spawn particle playing when a collision occursBouncy platform spawning inside playerBouncy platform sound being too low-pitchedVoice text not fully displaying before level endCursor being massive and blocking content on non-fullscreen mode


I have never made a complete pixel art game, and have little experience with 2D games on Unity.  This is my first game jam. Thank you very much for taking the time to play, and all feedback is appreciated!
Coming Soon:Ramping up the intensity a bit quickerMake object hitboxes bigger (this would also help mobile players!)Other thingss
Download Windows versionRight click and "Extract All"Open the unzipped folder, then navigate to and run "Blissfully Unaware.exe"Click more options and run anyway if prompted

game.short_text: Talking about the nature of failure
game.title: Crow's Counsel

details.title: Crow's Counsel
details.description:
Made over a week for Brackeys Game Jam

Summary:

Crow's  Counsel is a card-game where you combine  cards with different  stats to make the biggest number possible. Complete quests to progress and  hear more thoughts on failure from the handsome bird  who made the game.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Controls:
Next Dialogue - E/Space/Enter
Everything else - click it

Settings:
Sfx / Music volume:  It's volume. You know what that is.
Card Deal delay:  The amount of seconds before each card is dealt
Card Count delay:  The amount of seconds before each card is counted after round ended
Auto Dialogue delay:  The pause before auto dialogue moves is multiplied by this number
SILENCE, BIRD:  Silences the bird. (will skip all dialogue, doesn't take effect on dialogue that is already happening though.)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This is my first solo game jam! It was really stressful learning things I had never thought about until now under time pressure (those damn settings)
Nevertheless, it was tons of fun.
If you're interested, I usually work with my good friend Tidali, you can find our work here: CrowfoxCreatives

details.title: Wrong File
details.description:
Welcome to WF (Wrong File) Company—your dream job, where perfection is expected… until your computer turns into a virus battleground!
On your first week, you'll be busy working, all while secretly fending off rogue malware.
Type, click, and if you mess something up, fix it quietly before anyone notices.
Embrace the chaos!
How viruses work:
Trojan: No special behavior.
Pop-up: Spawns ads that accelerate the corruption process, close them quickly!
Worm: Moves through windows and minimize them, click on it to make it go back to its window!
Ransom: Locks away windows, solve math puzzles to unlock them!
Team:

Art: Potatotush & QuantumCrab

Some dialogues in this are generated by ChatGPT: The lines are prewritten in another language, and since English is not my first language, I put all the lines into Chatgpt to translate them.

One more round is a dungeon crawler, rogue-like game, where you, the player, is DRUNK! details.title: Perfect Game
details.description:
Controlls: click !anywhere! and drag. release to shoot the ball
Welcome to "The Perfect Game" – A Social Experiment in Gaming
Nothing Can Go Wrong... Right?

In this experimental game, you and other players from around the world will work together to create the perfect run.  But beware: every decision you make could change the course of the  game. Will you succeed, or will chaos take over? Let’s find out!

How It Works
The Run:
Each run consists of 5 levels. Your goal is to complete them and decide: Was that a perfect game?
If you think the run was perfect, vote "Yes". If not, vote "No" and replace one of the levels with your own creation.

The Community:
Every player experiences the same run. Your votes and level creations shape the game for everyone.
Once enough players agree that a run is perfect, a new run begins, and the cycle starts again.

Your Role:
Play: Complete the levels and share your opinion.
Create: Use the level editor to design your own levels and replace existing ones.
Vote: Decide whether the run is perfect or needs improvement.


The Experiment
This game is more than just a challenge – it’s a social experiment. Can a group of strangers work together to create something perfect? Or will chaos and unpredictability take over? Remember: Nothing can go wrong... or can it?

Get Started
Play the current run and see how far you can go.
Vote on whether the run is perfect.
Create your own levels and influence the game for everyone.
Watch as the community shapes the perfect run... or fails spectacularly.

Remember: Nothing Can Go Wrong
...but  if it does, it’s all part of the experiment. Let’s see what happens  when players take control. Are you ready to join the journey?

Key Features
Collaborative Gameplay: Work with players worldwide to create the perfect run.
Level Editor: Design and share your own levels.
Dynamic Runs: Every run is unique, shaped by the community.
Social Experiment: Discover what happens when players take control.
Adaptive Soundtrack!
Let’s Play!
Click Start to begin your journey. And remember: Nothing can go wrong... probably.

details.title: Intrusive Thoughts
details.description:
It's not a visual novel, it's not a dating simulator. It's what happened when the dialogue wheel from Mass effect met Undertale and they had a baby.
Controls:
Use WASD or the arrow keys to confirm dialogs and move your train of thought (represented as a little heart). Touching any color in the play area confirms a dialogue selection.
Press ESC to pause the game.
Credits:
Game design and programming: Josep VallsVisual design: https://softealpng.itch.io/Original soundtrack by https://ch3ckpo1nt.com/
Writing: http://alpha97.itch.io/
Other assets used:
https://itsu-saragi.itch.io/cafe-backgroundshttps://kmlgames.itch.io/friendly-scribbles
Gameplay notes:
What do the colors mean? (spoilers!)
Each color is associated with a "trait". The list below applies to the conversation, not the introductory cutscene:
* Red: self-centered vain
* Teal: self-conscious timid
* Blue: too-honest apathic
* Yellow: nervous jokester
* Purple: fake people-pleaser
* White: recovery from the last intrusive thought

Description!
Re-experience the thrilling times of participating in a game jam!

SHORT VIDEO GAMEPLAY WALKTHROUGH/GUIDE/TUTORIAL:

Link: https://youtu.be/m6KMPdJvjEI 

OPTIONAL HELP SECTION
If you know what you're doing, you can skip this next half!
--------------------------------------------------------------
Common misconceptions and misunderstandings players have faced:
COMMONLY REPORTED MISCONCEPTIONS:1. BROWSER VERSION of the game will have a SLIGHT LAG SPIKE at the very start.DOWNLOADABLE VERSION will run perfectly fine if needed.2. SOME KEYBOARDS are UNABLE to press specific 3-KEY COMBINATIONS.Don't worry, just PRESS AND HOLD the FIRST TWO keys.Then LET GO, you will then have 2 SECONDS to press the THIRD key.3. HEALTH REGEN, FIRE RATE, and SPAWN RATE, will all be FASTER when a LOWER value is applied, NOT a higher value.
If you are still facing any further issues with the controls:
MORE IN-DETAIL CONTROLS GUIDE1. KEYBOARD CONTROLS (ADDING MODULES)- PRESS AND HOLD the FIRST TWO KEYS inside the INSPIRATION MENU.- Then, LET GO of the keys.- Now you have 2 SECONDS to press the THIRD KEY from the CODE EDITOR.Still didn't work? Make sure these rules were applied:A. CORRECT COLOR:     - GREEN MODULES go to the TOWER SCRIPT     - RED MODULES go to the ENEMY SCRIPT     - BLUE MODULES go to the WORLD SCRIPTB. SLOT IS NOT ALREADY OCCUPIED:     - Only GRAY SLOTS(Default Values) can be added onto     - If it's NOT gray, use DEBUG MODE2. DEBUG MODE- You only need to press ONE KEY, which is the key of the module you wish to RESET INSIDE the CODE EDITOR- You can only reset NON-GRAY modules
What does "Balancing" the game mean? Here are the specifics:
1. Tower does not get DESTROYED- If the HEALTH of your tower hits 0, the win timer RESETS2. Tower must get HIT SOMETIMES- If the tower has not been HIT AT LEAST ONCE every 35 SECONDS, the win timer RESETS3. Enemies not WITHIN PLAY AREA- If your tower has not TARGETED AN ENEMY every 1.5 SECONDS, the win timer RESETS4. Tower is not HIT TOO MUCH- Regardless of health, if the tower has been HIT by 6 ENEMIES within a 10-SECOND TIME-FRAME (Last damaged still more than 25s), the win timer RESETS
My humblest apologies if you still face any troubles after this :(

details.description:
You've been entrusted with running a Space Program.  Design a rocket, input its flight plan, then launch it to try to reach a goal planet! Rockets cost a lot of money to make and fly, so your performance will be judged based on how expensive all of your attempts are. If you want the best score, nothing can go wrong!
1. Pick components on the left hand side and add them to your rocket. Rotate them to customize your design further.
2. Pick when components are active by adjusting their timeline on the bottom panel. Some missions will require components to turn on and off multiple times.
3. As a final step to preparation, click and drag on space to pick the launch direction of your rocket.
4. Click launch and watch your rocket soar! Or crash.

game.short_text: A platformer card game
game.title: BLIND FAITH

details.title: BLIND FAITH
details.description:
BLIND FAITH is an original card-based platformer created for Brackeys Game Jam 2025.1. Navigate through four challenging levels, but there’s a twist—your character is short-sighted, making movement a strategic puzzle.
Play cards to traverse the level, but be mindful of your choices. The minimap is only visible while discarding cards, forcing you to balance navigation with movement. Every action requires careful thinking and blind faith in your decisions.

If you plan well and give the right instructions... Nothing can go wrong.

PLAY TIME: 5-10 minutes

RECOMMENDED: Mouse and Keyboard.
HOW TO PLAY:

Description:
PLAY! / DISCARD! Button
This button changes the way you can interact with your cards and toggles the minimap on or off:
PLAY! MODE: The minimap is off.  When you click on a card your character performs that action if it can and you roll another card. Use this mode to traverse the world and keep your character safe.
DISCARD! MODE: The minimap is on. When you click on a card your character will not perform that action and you roll another card. Use this mode to plan your route and the cards you'll need.

CARDS
Stop, Go right, Go left, Dash, Jump, Wall Jump.
MINIMAP
The minimap will allow you to see a much broader view of the level.

details.description:
As the host of an evening party attended by the crème de la crème of society,

nothing can go wrong — nothing must go wrong. 
Your social standing is at stake, and only a flawless night will secure your place among the elite. 
Keep the guests entertained, the drinks flowing, and the conversation lively.
Oh, and don’t mention the war. 

How to play
The Soirée is a game of social interaction. Keep track of your guests' mood and cater to their preferences. If only they could ever agree on any one subject.
Use WASD or Arrow Keys to move around the room.Space or Right Click opens or closes the Context Sensitive Interactions Menu.Left Click on a Menu Item to select an Interaction. Make it 'till the end of the night.
You can access the Main Menu and Volume Settings at any time by pressing ESC.

Examples
of  situations you may encounter during the evening:

Be amused by the latest technology

Discuss the important matters in life

And if all else fails - get them drunk

details.title: Murph's
details.description:
Note: See known issues at the bottom.
If you run the quit command on the embedded web game, you'll have to refresh the webpage to restart.
--- 
Welcome to Murph's
You're thrown in the deep end on your first shift at Murph's. Learn how to navigate your computer; complete mini-game tasks to keep your mysterious stats above 0; and dig through the archive folder in your spare time to try to uncover the truth about this sinister company you're working for.
---
Tips
To just play the mini-games, you won't have too much trouble. Clicking on a task will tell you the exact usage you can do to open the app. Just type it into the console and hit enter.
If you're struggling, there are a few in-game files you can read to help you out. Here is a quick summary. Go explore in game for more.
README.txtBasic intro to the game and a hint towards where to look for the loreBASICS.txtHelp with how to use commands and how to navigate theEverything in the docs folderMore detailed information on tasks, commands, and appsInstructions on how to use the apps
There is an ending to the game! It's more than just the tasks and mini-games. There's some puzzles you'll have to solve. You might have to think outside the box here and there.
---
About the Game
This is my first game jam (Brackey's Game Jam 2025.1). I've started a whole bunch of projects and had lots of ideas. This game jam is the first time I've forced myself to get a more finished product out there. It's been a blast to create. I hope you can have some fun with it!
I've been enthralled by Ben Stiller's Severance on Apple TV recently. The game jam theme "Nothing can go wrong..." felt like it was perfect for a "mysterious and important", retro, dystopian, terminal game where the company punishes you for making mistakes. I have so many ideas with what I want to add from lore to apps/mini-games to effects. For those that like a bit of a puzzle, I hope you can enjoy digging around the computer to learn about the mysterious and sinister Murph's that you work for. For those that don't want to go digging, I hope the mini-games are fun enough on their own.
---
Known Issues
Console keeps focus on modal open[MAJOR] Some  paths don't work: absolute (e.g. ~/README.txt) or relative (e.g. ../README.txt). Hoping to fix this before the game jam deadline but it looks like a long fix so it so more major features are the priorityWindows
The game is self contained so just run the .exe
macOS
Coming soon hopefully

game.short_text: Survivorlike bullet hell where you become your own biggest enemy. Made for the Brackeys Game Jam 2025
game.title: BuggyBot

details.title: BuggyBot
details.description:
Feedback & Updates
 
Hey all, we've seen your feedback and also did some playtesting ourselves and decided to update the game to reflect that.
This includes the unified controls as well as the balance of the early game, but more on that at the bottom of this page...


Buggy Bot


In Buggy Bot you play as a tiny robot beetle, created by a very mad and very busy scientist.
Because he is a very mad and very busy scientist he has abandoned you alone in his backyard with all of his other creations.


Now you have to fend for yourself against all of the other mad denizens of this rusty old place full of trash.


Friendly Fire


Beware, the bullets you shoot to defend yourself from all the other crazy robots, that your creator has thrown into his back yard, bounce off of the trash lying around and might just come back to hit you instead.


Upgrades


In his merciful madness your scientist creator has given you the ability to assimilate items from the trash heap to upgrade your capabilities.


Risk-Reward System



Some upgrades can be very impactful to your enemies, but also yourself!
So make sure you choose wisely to stay in one piece for as long as you. How strong do you dare to get before you yourself become your biggest threat?


Leaderboard


Yeah, we've got one two :)


Day #0
Alternative Control Scheme

We have gotten a lot of feedback regarding the unified movement and shooting direction and while that decision was made to enforce the theme of the Jam, we too think that Buggy Bot can be a lot more fun with split controls.

Therefore we decided to offer a separate, more traditional, “Twin-Stick” control scheme version of Buggy Bot for both Windows and Linux, with split aim and movement directions using either 'WASD' for movement and the Mouse to aim or the Left&Right-Stick for controller input.
Due to this alternative control scheme having a large impact on the difficulty of the game, this version has a separate online leaderboard.


Day #1
Balance Patch

Since the end of the jam we have been busy playtesting, polishing and listening to your feedback on the balance of the game and found that especially the early game of our game tended to not be very exiting. It takes a while for your projectiles to actually pose a meaningful threat to yourself, especially with the split controls, and the enemies just take too long to destroy.
We also found that once you get to these later stages where your own projectiles pose a meaningful threat to yourself, you are basically not able to dodge them anymore, so we have decided to slow them down quite a bit.
All of these changes, as well as a couple other minor tweaks and bugfixes are available for download in the 'Rebalanced' version for both Windows and Linux. 


CREDITSArt : JassiBuum
Programming: Umbrason | Fabio Kolligs , Werghoul , SquashyHeemo , novalostj
Sound: Jelmerrr - Jelmer de Rooij
VoiceActing: JassiBuum, BudGarf

details.title: BRAINWORM
details.description:
SUMMARY
What is BRAINWORM?
BRAINWORM is a Snake-like arcade game where you control a parasitic worm devouring the brain of an up-and-coming presidential incumbent. Navigate obstacles like hardened brain tissue and deadly Macrophages ("Macs") as you sabotage your victim’s cognitive and speaking abilities. Do you have what it takes to cause debilitating brain damage?
CONTROLS
WASD (OR ARROW KEYS) - Movement

ESC/P - Pause Game

LMB (LEFT MOUSE BUTTON) - Select menu options
Game Jams (Brackeys & GWJ78)
Godot Wild Jam 78 - "Erase" & Brackeys Game Jam 2025.1 - "Nothing Can Go Wrong"
BRAINWORM was created for two game jams: Godot Wild Jam 78 (GWJ) and Brackeys Game Jam 2025.1 (BGJ). Inspired by both themes, the duo at Hangover Sunshine explored the concept of a brainworm "erasing" the cognitive functions of a rising politician. The classic Snake gameplay was chosen for its resemblance to worms and its emphasis on avoiding mistakes—echoing the idea that "nothing can go wrong."
Spoiler alert: After the brain-eaten politician becomes president, players are left to wonder: did nothing go wrong because the worm ate everything, or because even without a brain, the candidate’s path to power remained unimpeded?
Tools Used
Godot 4.2
Procreate 
Photoshop (adjusting sprites)
FL Studio 21
Audacity
Content Warning
BRAINWORM may have themes and gameplay that may be triggering. Please review the content warnings below before continuing:

Death and/or dying,
Mild cartoon violence,
Blood, and/or gore (hemophobia),
Depiction of cognitive decline (dementophobia),
Depiction of anti-science sentiment & general misinformation.
Attributions

My submission to the 2025.1 Brackeys Game Jam

Press Esc at any time to go back to the main menu
This is the shortest time I've ever had to make a game for a jam and I really enjoyed it! Definitely coming back for the next one.Feel free to give feedback and tell me if you find any bugs as I'll probably come back and make a patch update once the jam is over.
Post-Jam Update 1.0:
Thank you so much to everyone who played my game,this was my first time doing a competitive jam like this and I could've never imagined it going this well. There were a lot of really great interpretations of the theme many of which probably deserve first place much more than I do. I've listed a few of them here though keep in mind this is just out of the ones that I've played.Powderpath by Lizardlike- I thought a person made of gunpowder was a really funny sarcastic interpretation of the themeFlorordination by Tey- Really well constructed story around the theme that made the gameplay pretty tense as well
Only Right by ForingSan- Also used the idea of everything moving right but also combined it with the original idea of nothing going wrong in a really clever way

Game Changes:
I fixed a couple bugs people mentioned or I never got around to, namely getting stuck inside the portals  and the portals breaking when the player moves too fast.Added the option to navigate the main menu using keyboard controls,planning to do this for other menus as wellMade the time trial mode look nicerI had planned on adding more levels with a new mechanic, though unfortunately I couldn't get it to work in time.I guess it's Left up to your imagination for now :)

You need to fix or stop events that make bridezilla's wedding anything but perfect.

(Mouse click and drag)
Win: Survive Bridezilla's Wedding
Lose: Get Hit by an attack by an Enraged Bridezilla.

details.title: One Set Wonder
details.description:
DISCOVER HIDDEN COMBOS
HYPE THE CROWD
SAVE THE PARTY
Welcome to One Set Wonder! Do you have what it takes to take over the DJ booth while the regular DJ takes a call in the other room? Thankfully, it looks like the crowds pretty thin, so if you think about it... nothing can go wrong! At least the DJ left some inspiration stuck to the table ;)
Controls: Space bar to "HYPE the crowd" and num keys 1-4 to play the synth hits. Both of these can be used in time with the beat, even if there aren't specifically synth notes on the board! You can also click to interact with the DJ kit in front of you. 
Goal: Build up as much hype as possible by finding combos hidden in the environment and unlocking riffs. The audience hype is displayed on the bar at the top. Pay attention to the environment to find inspiration for what to play if you feel stuck (start with the post-it note)! Experiment with tempo, FX switches, EQ knobs, and record scratching!

Due to a slight engine related audio issue, the web version of this game (though very playable) may tend to slip off beat slightly more often than its desktop counterpart. We recommend you download and run OneSetWonder.exe for the optimal way to play.
WARNING: This game contains flashing lights. There is an option on the starting screen to enable photosensitive mode which disables most of the lighting effects.

ATLAS
ATLAS is an adventure game developed by four students in Japan. 
You explore the countryside's nature and solve the map's mystery.

How to Play
Press on the file with the "exe" or "app" extension.

Left Click...Move camera, Interact with an object.
Right Click...Hide UI.
Click Map...Move to other area.
Click Note...Take notes.

For the best experience, we recommend playing it in Full HD on a PC.
